description.p: Super Productivity is an open-source to-do list and time tracker for developers, freelancers, and anyone managing complex personal workflows. It works fully offline, with no account required.

ul.li: Drag-and-drop daily planner, weekly schedule, and Kanban boards
Built-in Pomodoro, timeboxing, and Flowtime modes with one-click time tracking and reports
Integrations with Jira, GitHub, GitLab, Gitea, Redmine, OpenProject, and Google Calendar
Optional sync via Nextcloud, WebDAV, Dropbox, or Super Sync; runs fully offline by default
Automations: rule-based triggers that auto-tag, auto-move, or auto-start tracking
Deadlines: sort, group, and filter tasks by deadline
Procrastination helper with CBT techniques and a structured end-of-day review
Plugin system with community plugins for Obsidian, Google Calendar, and more
Onboarding presets at first launch: Simple Todo, Time Tracker, or full Productivity Suite
Privacy by default: no sign-up, no tracking, no telemetry; MIT-licensed

description.p: Super Productivity has been in active development since 2016 and is supported entirely by community contributions and optional GitHub Sponsorships.

screenshot.caption: Today view: scheduled tasks, current task, and time tracked
Kanban board with drag-and-drop columns and per-project layouts
Weekly planner with tasks and calendar events
Schedule view with synced calendar events and tasks for the day
Inbox for quick capture of tasks, notes, and bookmarks

ul.li: GitHub provider: optional inclusion of pull requests in issues
Focus mode: enlarged timer and reorganized Pomodoro controls
Plugin API: new reInitData hook
Sync: smoother Dropbox auth on sandboxed Linux
Stability and translation improvements
Boards: tag AND/OR match mode and new sort options
Focus mode: pomodoro session counter reset button
Android: WebView crash recovery and reliable alarm restoration after updates
Sync: copy-URL fallback for Dropbox auth and restored periodic timer for file-based providers
Detect legacy v16.x sync format and warn instead of diverging silently
New CalDAV Calendar plugin with time-blocking support
Google Calendar plugin: reschedule, delete, and auto time-blocking
Local REST API for desktop automation
Conditional plugin config fields (showIf)
Better input handling for hybrid touch devices
Automations: rule-based triggers (auto-tag, auto-move, auto-start tracking)
Onboarding presets at first launch: Simple Todo, Time Tracker, or Productivity Suite
Deadlines: sort, group, and filter tasks by deadline
Zen theme: minimal, calmer UI with transparent backgrounds
Mobile parity: swipe gestures, Y-axis-locked drag-and-drop, background sync on Android
Clickable URLs in task titles
Bundled Google Calendar provider plugin
Sync engine reliability: fixed false "in sync" status and DELETE vs UPDATE conflict races
Mobile: swipe-to-close gesture for side navigation
Planner: limit initial days to 5 on mobile for faster startup
SuperSync: encryption-at-rest tooling and Docker monitoring
Schedule view: drag-and-drop scheduling rework with new right-panel
Schedule: better drop preview, touch support, and panel scaling
Schedule: panel auto-refresh every two minutes
